mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets compress wage growth and trigger inflation expectations, prompting the Federal Reserve to pause rate cuts and causing equity valuations to contract over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0%, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive 3-month FEDFUNDS increases, invalidating the rate-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.5% indicates an overheating economy that historically precedes Federal Reserve policy pivots, causing equity markets to price in higher discount rates and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth combined with stagnant short-term rates signals accommodative monetary policy that fuels risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of historical episodes, or the interaction coefficient loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the industrial production index reflect weakening aggregate supply conditions that historically precede recessionary earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy remains below 50% over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between declining and rising INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.8% year-over-year, inflation fears trigger repricing of long-duration equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 2.8%, or the signal's predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all out-of-sample windows.
